name: bun
description: A fast bundler, transpiler, JavaScript Runtime and package manager for web software

subcommands.name: c
subcommands.description: Start a new project from a template

name: --open
options.description: On finish, start bun and open in browser

args.name: template
args.description: Package from @bun-examples, GitHub repo, or local file

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a package.json script or executable

script: until [[ -f package.json ]] || [[ $PWD = '/' ]]; do cd ..; done; cat package.json

name: -f
--force
options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies

name: --cache-dir
options.description: Store & load cached data from a specific directory path

name: --backend
options.description: Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'

name: --link-native-bins
options.description: Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo

name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages

name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

name: -f
--force
options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies

name: --cache-dir
options.description: Store & load cached data from a specific directory path

name: --backend
options.description: Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'

name: --link-native-bins
options.description: Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo

name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages

name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

name: -f
--force
options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies

name: --cache-dir
options.description: Store & load cached data from a specific directory path

name: --backend
options.description: Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'

name: --link-native-bins
options.description: Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo

name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages

name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Bundle files using Bun's native bundler

name: -b
--bun
options.description: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)

args.name: path
args.description: Absolute path to resolve files & entry points from. This just changes the process' cwd

args.name: path
args.description: Config file to load Bun from (e.g. -c bunfig.toml)

args.name: order
args.description: Defaults to: .tsx,.ts,.jsx,.js,.json

name: --jsx-factory
options.description: Changes the function called when compiling JSX elements using the classic JSX runtime

name: --jsx-fragment
options.description: Changes the function called when compiling JSX fragments

name: --jsx-import-source
options.description: Declares the module specifier to be used for importing the jsx and jsxs factory functions. Default: "react"

args.name: args
args.description: Import a module before other modules are loaded

args.name: args
args.description: Main fields to lookup in package.json. Defaults to --target dependent

name: --revision
options.description: Print version with revision and exit

args.name: overrides
args.description: Load tsconfig from path instead of cwd/tsconfig.json

args.name: k=v
args.description: Substitute key=value while parsing, e.g. --define process.env.NODE_ENV=development

args.name: args
args.description: Exclude module from transpilation (can use * wildcards). ex: -e react

args.name: args
args.description: Parse files with .ext:loader, e.g. --loader .js:jsx. Valid loaders: js, jsx, ts, tsx, json, toml, text, file, wasm, napi

args.name: args
args.description: Rewrite import URLs to start with --origin. Default: ""

args.name: args
args.description: Port to serve Bun's dev server on. Default: "3000"

name: --smol
options.description: Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often

name: --no-macros
options.description: Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime

name: --inspect
options.description: Activate Bun's debugger for a file

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --inspect-wait
options.description: Activate Bun's Debugger, wait for a connection before executing

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --inspect-brk
options.description: Activate Bun's Debugger, set breakpoint on first line of code and wait

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --if-present
options.description: Exit if the entrypoint does not exist

name: --hot
options.description: Enable auto reload in the Bun runtime, test runner, or bundler

name: --watch
options.description: Automatically restart the process on file change

name: --no-install
options.description: Disable auto install in the Bun runtime

name: -i
options.description: Automatically install dependencies and use global cache in Bun's runtime, equivalent to --install=fallback

args.name: arg
args.description: Install dependencies automatically when no node_modules are present, default: "auto". "force" to ignore node_modules, fallback to install any missing

name: --prefer-offline
options.description: Skip staleness checks for packages in the Bun runtime and resolve from disk

name: --prefer-latest
options.description: Use the latest matching versions of packages in the Bun runtime, always checking npm

name: --silent
options.description: Don't repeat the command for bun run

name: compile
options.description: Generate a standalone Bun executable containing your bundled code

name: --format
options.description: Specifies the module format to build to. Only esm is supported currently

name: --root
options.description: Root directory used for multiple entry points

name: --public-path
options.description: A prefix to be appended to any import paths in bundled code

name: --entry-naming
options.description: Customize entry point filenames. Defaults to "[dir]/[name].[ext]"

name: --no-bundle
options.description: Transpile file only, do not bundle

name: --dump-environment-variables
options.description: Dump environment variables from .env and process as JSON and quit. Useful for debugging

name: --dump-limits
options.description: Dump system limits. Useful for debugging

args.name: entrypoints
args.description: Entrypoint to bundle. If multiple entrypoints provided, must specify --outdir

name: -f
--force
options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies

name: --cache-dir
options.description: Store & load cached data from a specific directory path

name: --backend
options.description: Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'

name: --link-native-bins
options.description: Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo

name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages

name: --ignore-scripts
options.description: Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Run without an argument to register this package to the global package registry. Uses the name field from package.json

args.name: package
args.description: Install a package from the global package registry

subcommands.name: unlink
subcommands.description: Unlink this package from the global package registry

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Get the latest version of bun

name: -b
--bun
options.description: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)

args.description: Absolute path to resolve files & entry points from. This just changes the process' cwd

name: --smol
options.description: Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often

name: --no-macros
options.description: Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime

name: --inspect
options.description: Activate Bun's debugger for a file

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --inspect-wait
options.description: Activate Bun's Debugger, wait for a connection before executing

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --inspect-brk
options.description: Activate Bun's Debugger, set breakpoint on first line of code and wait

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname

name: --if-present
options.description: Exit if the entrypoint does not exist

name: --hot
options.description: Enable auto reload in the Bun runtime, test runner, or bundler

name: --watch
options.description: Automatically restart the process on file change

name: --no-install
options.description: Disable auto install in the Bun runtime

name: -i
options.description: Automatically install dependencies and use global cache in Bun's runtime, equivalent to --install=fallback

args.name: arg
args.description: Install dependencies automatically when no node_modules are present, default: "auto". "force" to ignore node_modules, fallback to install any missing

name: --prefer-offline
options.description: Skip staleness checks for packages in the Bun runtime and resolve from disk

name: --prefer-latest
options.description: Use the latest matching versions of packages in the Bun runtime, always checking npm

name: --silent
options.description: Don't repeat the command for bun run

name: --timeout
options.description: Set the per-test timeout in milliseconds, default is 5000

name: --rerun-each
options.description: Re-run each test file <NUMBER> times, helps catch certain bugs

name: --only
options.description: Only run tests that are marked with "test.only()"

name: --todo
options.description: Include tests that are marked with "test.todo()"

name: --bail
options.description: Exit the test suite after <NUMBER> failures. If you do not specify a number, it defaults to 1

name: -t
--test-name-pattern
options.description: Run only tests with a name that matches the given regex

name: --dump-environment-variables
options.description: Dump environment variables from .env and process as JSON and quit. Useful for debugging

name: --dump-limits
options.description: Dump system limits. Useful for debugging

args.name: files
args.description: Test files to run

script: find $(npm prefix) | grep -E ".*.(test|_test|spec|_spec).(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$" | grep -vE ".*/node_modules/.*" | sed "s|$(npm prefix)/||"

subcommands.name: pm
subcommands.description: Set of utilities for working with Bun's package manager

name: bin
options.description: Print the path to bin folder

name: cache
options.description: Print the path to the cache folder

name: hash
options.description: Generate & print the hash of the current lockfile

name: hash-print
options.description: Print the hash stored in the current lockfile

name: hash-string
options.description: Print the string used to hash the lockfile

name: ls
options.description: List the dependency tree according to the current lockfile

args.name: command
args.description: The command to run

script: until [[ -d node_modules/ ]] || [[ $PWD = '/' ]]; do cd ..; done; ls -1 node_modules/.bin/`

subcommands.name: repl
subcommands.description: Run a REPL (read eval print loop) with the Bun runtime.(experimental)

name: --inspect
options.description: Activate Bun's debugger for a file

args.name: [host:]port
args.description: Activate inspector on particular port and/or hostname
